summary: Governance state lives in three repo instruments — an overwritable present-mirror, an append-only history log, and an append-only authority-approval register — kept true by a mandatory session close ritual.

rules.rule: Every session closes by synchronizing the three instruments to reality: update the present-mirror, append what the session did and why to the log, and add a register row if an authority decision was granted — if you did not update the mirror, you did not close.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When the present-mirror and reality disagree, reality wins and the mirror is corrected — never the other way around.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never edit away or rewrite entries in the append-only log or the decision register — entries are added below, never edited; only the present-mirror is overwritable.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Record every authority-class approval as a register row the moment it is granted — with who proposed, authority, when, what, channel, and evidence — not reconstructed later.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never collapse the three instruments into one another: merging the mirror into the log loses the single-present view, merging the register into the log loses auditable provenance, and merging the register into the ADR conflates authorization with design rationale.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: The consuming repo's L3 binding instantiates file names, authority classes, channels, and evidence conventions but never re-writes the instrument roles, the mirror-wins rule, the register field set, or the close ritual — repeating them is drift.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
